 {"id":"ft-v0339","title":"[MEDIUM] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology post-rollback check only compares pane-id set, not tree shape","description":"Commit 30172171 fixed ft-olq2e by routing the post-rollback check through server.registry().snapshot() instead of the vacuous re-deserialization of initial_topo_json. However the new primary assertion at integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs still only compares PANE IDs:

  post_rollback_pane_ids_sorted == baseline_pane_ids

TopologySnapshot (crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs) is a tree of windows → tabs → panes, with per-window position/size/title and per-tab pane layout metadata. The registry snapshot, filtered to LifecycleEntityKind::Pane, flattens all of that to a bag of pane u64 ids.

Consequence: a RemoteRequest::Rollback that restores the correct set of pane entities but (a) reparents them under the wrong tab, (b) drops a window or tab node, (c) corrupts the split tree under a tab, or (d) restores panes in the wrong order will still pass the assertion. Rollback tree-shape correctness is therefore still not covered by this test — the bead it closes was motivated precisely by false confidence in rollback behavior.

Expected fix:
- Reconstruct a post-rollback TopologySnapshot from the live server (registry + whatever stores tab/window edges) and compare it field-by-field against the pre-expansion baseline — at least window ids + tab ids + pane ids grouped by tab.
- Keep the pane-id-set assertion as a weaker redundant guard.

Regression risk: medium. The test name promises topology validation; reviewers relying on this bead's closure will assume tree-structure regressions are caught when in fact only pane existence is.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:21.495704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017561Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017321Z","close_reason":"Strengthened rollback validation to require exact TopologySnapshot restoration, not just pane-id/fingerprint agreement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-v2ifl","title":"[HIGH] AGENTS.md does not disclose NTM-gap command families (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile all return robot.not_implemented)","description":"Reality-check audit F1: crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:22887/22946/22993/23071/23131 all dispatch through build_ntm_not_implemented_response returning robot.not_implemented. README line 586-590 discloses this; AGENTS.md does NOT. An agent reading AGENTS.md in isolation will try these families and be surprised. Fix: add a 'Not yet implemented' section to AGENTS.md listing these 5 families.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:00:55.723853Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:01:55.919697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:01:55.919438Z","close_reason":"eb348213 adds 'Not Yet Implemented' section to AGENTS.md between Robot Mode API and Session Persistence; cites main.rs:22887/22946/22993/23071/23131 and README:586.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","reality-check"]}
 {"id":"ft-v3ob9","title":"[perf] ingest discovery_tick does redundant map probes and allocates closed-pane list every poll","description":"Hot path in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:948-1037. PaneRegistry::discovery_tick() does get_mut() + contains_key() + get() on the same HashMap entry for existing panes, clones PaneInfo just to call update_info(), and allocates a closed Vec<u64> by scanning all keys after each discovery poll. With large pane fleets and short poll intervals this compounds into avoidable hashing and allocation churn on every tick. Fix by collapsing the existing/new pane branches to a single entry-local flow, moving PaneInfo into update_info without cloning, and replacing the temporary closed vector with an in-place drain/remove pass or reusable scratch storage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:02.217684Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:19:52.533100Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:19:52.532943Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3684e17d: collapsed discovery_tick map probes and reused closed-pane scratch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"]}

Replace with shared storage test support after ft-dn2tu.1.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:15.771652Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-29T19:43:18.929191Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T19:43:18.928932Z","close_reason":"backpressure_integration_tests now uses shared storage runtime helper","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-v5do5","title":"scan_pipeline: per-category trigger count parity not pinned (only totals compared)","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_scan_pipeline.rs` has three chunked-vs-batch trigger parity properties:
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity (line 206)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries (line 229)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries_with_compression (line 255)

All three assert only `batch_total == chunked_total` via `total_matches`. None compare the per-category distribution (`TriggerScanResult.counts`).

A future refactor that miscounts one category and compensates in another — e.g., an off-by-one in overlap skip that drops an Error match while spuriously counting a Warning — would pass all three existing tests while quietly corrupting per-category analytics (rate-limit detection, error clustering, workflow routing).

# Fix

Add `chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity` proptest: assert `batch_output.triggers.counts == chunked_output.triggers.counts` (HashMap equality) across arbitrary chunk boundaries. Keeps the definitive-rescan invariant honest at the category level, not just the aggregate level.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect, just coverage thinness. Shipping as a feature/test strengthening rather than a bug fix because the current code path IS correct (flush re-scans with the same scan_counts used by batch, so per-category counts MUST match); the test just doesn't pin it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:02.169355Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:36.447073Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:36.446610Z","close_reason":"Landed in 59ba4ceb — two new proptests (chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity + *_with_compression) assert batch.counts == chunked.counts HashMap equality across arbitrary chunk boundaries, closing the total-only gap in the existing parity suite. Verified: 2 passed, 0 failed, 256 cases each.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-v5lz3","title":"[postswarm] 2026-04-27 follow-ups: cobalt disposition + GPU harness + memory GC + operator tooling + orchestration playbook","description":"# [postswarm] 2026-04-27 follow-ups — tracking epic

## Background
On 2026-04-27 a 16-tick swarm session (2 cc + 4 cod + 1 user pane, plus an
operator agent driving 4-minute ticks) ran from 18:48Z to 20:38Z, producing
35 commits and closing 16 beads. The session began with 4 ready beads
(ft-1memj.28, wa-nu4.3.9, ft-6scm7, ft-z1809), ran through review
saturation, then escalated to a FINAL exploratory mode that dispatched one
of {/security-audit-for-saas, /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis,
/profiling-software-performance, /deadlock-finder-and-fixer,
/mock-code-finder, /reality-check-for-project} per pane. That FINAL pass
filed 13 new beads, almost all of which were claimed and closed within
the same session (ft-lv819, ft-psa5d, ft-9ey46, ft-jehxh, ft-besoo,
ft-okhhj, ft-iqwt5, ft-xg8st, ft-hdvvo, ft-d3awp, ft-mt6uv, ft-thj9b,
ft-h35n4, ft-npobg, ft-5o6u5).

This tracking epic captures the small set of follow-ups that did NOT close
inside that session and the operational improvements that emerged from
running the swarm itself.

## Session-end state at 2026-04-27T20:38Z
- 35 commits total (newest 25: bf9db5d5, 76d82223, 056b58e0, 069db5ce,
  3fee5c13, 1658d0f6, d858725c, d05e5b7b, 6a0590d2, 82c8aa78, 9407287e,
  17bea2d5, a6e0c2cd, a64810e4, c1aea540, 652600f4, 2e51b631, 15223aed,
  b665e256, 46cdcffe, afd692a3, d2525149, 719a05d7, 357fb60c, 25bb607a).
- 16 beads closed.
- 1 new bead filed and not yet started: ft-ombfl (P2 GPU regression
  harness follow-on for ft-1memj.28).
- 1 epic still in_progress under an unresponsive assignee: wa-nu4.3.9
  (codex-cobalt, claimed 1h36m+, last commit 25bb607a was 36m before the
  session ended).
- 1 strategic epic in_progress under the user: ft-y0loj (out of scope
  for this tracking epic).
- 3 STALE memory entries flagged in MEMORY.md (patterns_sweep,
  capture_storage_sweep, mux_term_codec_sweep).
- 2 ad-hoc operator scripts committed in 3fee5c13:
  scripts/swarm-tick.sh + scripts/clean-stale-targets.sh.

## Why this epic exists (overarching project goals it serves)
1. frankenterm is a swarm-native control plane for AI agent fleets. The
   operator-tooling improvements directly serve dogfooding the product on
   its own development. A flaky swarm-tick or untested cleanup script
   risks losing a real swarm session to a regression in the operator
   layer itself.
2. The headless visual GPU regression harness is the single biggest
   missing quality gate for the GUI binary. ft-1memj.28 originally
   covered both the divider geometry test (shipped as bf9db5d5) and the
   broader visual-suite — the broader piece spilled to ft-ombfl. Without
   this harness, regressions in shaping (ft-6scm7-class), cursor
   rendering, selection, multi-pane layout, and emoji fallback can ship
   undetected because there is no machine-checkable golden.
3. Documenting the orchestration playbook in AGENTS.md compounds:
   every future swarm benefits from "don't use --robot-interrupt
   --interrupt-msg" and "cc panes need explicit Enter".
4. Stale memory entries inflate the auto-loaded context cost every
   session and risk agents reasoning over outdated facts. The "STALE"
   self-tag mechanism only works if we actually GC at the threshold.
5. wa-nu4.3.9 ship-readiness is on the v0.1.0 critical path. Leaving it
   in_progress under an absent assignee blocks the release.

## Scope (what this epic tracks)
1. **cobalt-disposition** — audit wa-nu4.3.9 vs delivered commits;
   either close it with evidence or decompose remaining deliverables
   into independently claimable child beads.
2. **GPU regression harness** — child beads under ft-ombfl: design doc,
   harness scaffold, renderer integration, fixture corpus (text, CJK,
   emoji+fallback, box-drawing, cursor states, selection overlay,
   multi-pane), frame-time reporter, comparator unit tests, e2e script
   with structured logging, CI wiring, stress tests.
3. **Memory GC** — re-verify and remove three stale subsystem-sweep
   memories (or, if any new finding emerges, file P1 audit beads
   instead of GC'ing).
4. **Operator tooling hardening** — unit tests for swarm-tick.sh and
   clean-stale-targets.sh; shellcheck CI gate; runbook doc; optional
   ntm-coordinator integration.
5. **Orchestration playbook** — AGENTS.md addendum capturing the
   ntm/codex/cc submit-behavior lessons and the convergence-test
   calibration adjustment that the session validated empirically.

## Out of scope
- ft-y0loj sub-extraction work (the user-owned strategic epic; its own
  10 child beads track that work independently).
- Beads closed during the session itself.
- Any new feature work surfaced by post-session brainstorming that did
  not arise from the swarm's findings.

## Definition of done (this epic)
- All children closed.
- `br dep cycles` returns empty.
- A verification commit lands documenting which child closed which
  deliverable, and pointing at this bead.
- bv --robot-triage shows zero remaining open beads tagged with the
  postswarm-2026-04-27 label after closure.

## Acceptance criteria (top-level)
- wa-nu4.3.9 is either closed with auditable evidence its scope was met,
  OR decomposed into named child beads with codex-cobalt cleared as
  assignee on the parent.
- ft-ombfl has a passing golden-snapshot harness with all listed
  surfaces covered, frame-time reporter integrated, and CI wiring
  uploading diff artifacts on failure.
- AGENTS.md contains the orchestration playbook addendum, verified by
  diff and by a swarm session reading it without surprise.
- The three flagged stale memories are GC'd (or replaced with new
  audit beads).
- scripts/swarm-tick.sh and scripts/clean-stale-targets.sh have unit
  tests passing in CI, and shellcheck runs clean.

## Risks / considerations
- The GPU harness work is the largest sub-tree. Be careful to scope it
  so that fixtures can be parallelized across panes — keep each fixture
  bead independently claimable.
- AGENTS.md edits are touchy because Rule 0 of that file is the
  fundamental-override-prerogative. Stay strictly within the
  orchestration-playbook addendum and do not edit unrelated rules.
- Memory GC requires re-running each sweep tool to confirm "still clean"
  before deleting. Skipping the re-verify and just GC'ing risks losing a
  finding that materialized after the threshold.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- tracking-epic

## References
- Session summary: chat at 2026-04-27T20:38Z (newest 25 commits enumerated).
- Operator scripts: scripts/swarm-tick.sh, scripts/clean-stale-targets.sh
  (committed 3fee5c13).
- ft-ombfl (existing P2 bead — children attached here).
- wa-nu4.3.9 (existing P2 epic — disposition tracked here).
- MEMORY.md at /Users/jemanuel/.claude/projects/-Users-jemanuel-projects-frankenterm/memory/.","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (operator-script generality + CI retention spec)

### Operator scripts must accept project path as arg
The current scripts/swarm-tick.sh hardcodes
/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm. This blocks reuse across
projects (the very dogfooding scenario the swarm-tick tests
mention multiple sessions running in parallel).

Add to OT_TICK + OT_CLEAN deliverable scope (cross-ref via
their --notes):
- Accept project path as positional arg or PROJECT_PATH env var.
- Default to $(pwd) if neither given.
- Validate the path is a git repo (contains .git/).
- Reject paths outside ~/projects/ unless --force flag (safety
  for clean-stale).

This is a small but important generalization. File as a
sub-deliverable of OT_TICK and OT_CLEAN (NOT a new bead — the
test suites already cover the scripts, just expand fixtures).

### CI artifact retention (concrete)
For all postswarm work that uploads CI artifacts:
- PR-level artifacts: 7 days retention.
- Main-branch artifacts: 30 days retention.
- Release-tag artifacts: 90 days retention.
- Stress / perf-baseline artifacts: keep indefinitely (mark
  with `name: perf-baseline-${{ github.sha }}` and never expire).

Set via:
```yaml
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: gpu-harness-diffs
    path: /tmp/gpu-harness-*/diffs/
    retention-days: 7
```
Default GitHub Actions retention is 90; explicit short retention
saves storage cost.

### Cross-link to ft-y0loj
ROOT now has a `related` dep to ft-y0loj (added in this polish
round). Future operators inspecting the dep tree see ft-y0loj's
strategic importance. ft-y0loj is the user-owned strategic epic
for frankenterm-core sub-extraction; out of scope for this
tracking epic but referenced for context.

### Cross-link to wa-vv3h (project rename)
Cobalt-disposition (ft-v5lz3.4) has a `related` dep to wa-vv3h.
This is the project-rename coordinator. If the rename lands
during a postswarm work cycle, CI workflow names and binary
references in the operator scripts must be updated atomically
with the rename PR.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:56:01.604291Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:03:31.506811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["postswarm-2026-04-27","tracking-epic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-v5lz3","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:12:13.839564Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-v5lz3","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-28T02:01:46.636925Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3652,"issue_id":"ft-v5lz3","author":"jemanuel","text":"[ft-v5lz3 audit by cc_2 — 2026-04-28T05:00Z]

Status: cannot claim — beads CLI rejects with `cannot claim blocked

Child audit (16 of 17 closed):
  ft-v5lz3.1     closed  — mem-gc parent
  ft-v5lz3.1.1   closed  — patterns sweep memory GC verified clean
  ft-v5lz3.1.2   closed  — capture/storage sweep memory GC verified clean

 {"id":"ft-v5lz3.1","title":"[postswarm] GC three stale subsystem-sweep memories from MEMORY.md (patterns / capture-storage / mux-term-codec)","description":"# GC stale subsystem-sweep memories — root

## Background
MEMORY.md at /Users/jemanuel/.claude/projects/-Users-jemanuel-projects-frankenterm/memory/
is auto-loaded into every session's context. As of 2026-04-27 it
explicitly self-tags three subsystem-sweep memories as STALE per their
own threshold logic:

- patterns_sweep_findings.md — STALE (24 commits since SHA 2ca20e18,
  threshold 5; tracked under ft-hph8i / ft-nedq3).
- capture_storage_sweep_findings.md — STALE (154 commits since SHA
  f5a37ec9, threshold 30; tracked under ft-hph8i / ft-nedq3).
- mux_term_codec_sweep.md — STALE (91 commits since SHA 312f06b9,
  threshold 20; tracked under ft-hph8i / ft-nedq3).

These sweeps reported "0 cat-A findings" at their respective base SHAs;
they were audit milestones, not action items. Carrying them past their
freshness threshold inflates context cost every session and conditions
agents to reason over data older than the project's actual state.

## Why this matters (overarching project goals)
- Memory budget: every session reads MEMORY.md early; trimming stale
  entries reduces auto-loaded context cost (currently ~33.5KB / 354
  lines per the memory header) and makes the live entries more
  prominent.
- Trust: stale entries linger → agents start mistrusting other entries
  by association.
- Process: the stale-tag mechanism only works if we actually GC at the
  threshold. Letting it slip teaches agents the threshold is fictional
  and degrades the signal.

## Scope (this is the ROOT bead with three children)
Three children, one per memory entry. Each child:
1. Re-runs the relevant sweep tool/check on current HEAD.
2. If still 0 cat-A findings:
   - Removes the per-topic .md file from the memory directory.
   - Removes its bullet line from MEMORY.md.
3. If new findings:
   - Files a P1/P2 audit bead with the finding(s).
   - Updates the MEMORY.md bullet to point at the new audit bead and
     advance the SHA + threshold.

## Out of scope
- Other (non-stale) MEMORY.md entries. The stale-tag is the gate.
- New audit work beyond confirming "still clean" or filing the new
  audit bead from a confirmed finding.

## Approach (per-child, common shape)
1. Read the current memory file and capture its base SHA + threshold.
2. `git log --oneline <base-SHA>..HEAD -- <relevant-paths>` to surface
   what changed since the threshold base.
3. Re-apply the original audit method:
   - For "mock-finder" sweeps: rg patterns for unimplemented!()/todo!()/
     panic!("not implemented")/Ok(())-with-no-work/FIXME/STUB/MOCK/
     PLACEHOLDER outside #[cfg(test)].
   - For sweep-style audits: read the original memory file's "Method"
     section if present and reproduce.
4. If clean: delete file + remove MEMORY.md line.
5. If finding: file new bead, update MEMORY.md line.
6. Verify: `cat MEMORY.md | grep <topic>` returns expected.

## Acceptance criteria (root)
- All three children closed.
- MEMORY.md no longer contains the three flagged stale-bullet lines
  (unless replaced with new audit-bead pointers).
- The corresponding .md files are removed from the memory directory.
- `bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'` is empty.

## Testing
- Per-child verification commands documented in each child's
  description.
- Final `cat MEMORY.md | grep -E 'patterns_sweep|capture_storage|mux_term_codec'`
  returns empty (or only references new audit beads).
- `ls /Users/jemanuel/.claude/projects/-Users-jemanuel-projects-frankenterm/memory/project_*sweep*.md`
  returns empty.

## Risks / considerations
- AGENTS.md Rule 1 forbids file deletion without permission. Memory
  files live OUTSIDE the project repo and the auto-memory system
  prompt explicitly contemplates removal of outdated entries ("Update
  or remove memories that turn out to be wrong or outdated"). Children
  must explicitly call out that this exception applies and not
  generalize it.
- Don't GC a memory whose audit no longer holds — re-verify before
  delete. The cost of re-running the sweep is small; the cost of
  losing a finding is large.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- memory-gc
- chore

## References
- MEMORY.md at /Users/jemanuel/.claude/projects/-Users-jemanuel-projects-frankenterm/memory/
- Auto-memory rules in the system prompt (write/access semantics).
- AGENTS.md Rule 1 (no file deletion without permission — exception
  scope clarified per memory-system semantics).
- Existing audit beads: ft-hph8i, ft-nedq3.","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (claim protocol for derived audit beads)

### When a memory-GC child surfaces a NEW finding
The child files a new audit bead (P1 cat-A, P3 cat-B per round-2
notes). But the child agent is not necessarily the right
implementer for the fix. Protocol:

1. **Filer is NOT the closer.** The agent that finds the
   regression files the bead, then continues with their original
   GC scope (e.g., still GCs the memory entry but with a
   "replaced with bead X" pointer).
2. **The new audit bead becomes ready immediately.** It's a
   normal P1/P3 bead under the project root, NOT a child of
   the GC tree (so it's discoverable independently).
3. **Cross-reference both directions:** the new audit bead
   has a `related` dep to the GC child that found it; the GC
   child appends a notes line `Filed audit bead: <id>`.
4. **Priority discipline:**
   - cat-A finding (production stub/regression): P1 BUG.
   - cat-B finding (style/refactor): P3 TASK.
   - cat-C finding (interesting but unactionable): note in GC
     child's `--notes`, do NOT file new bead.

### What about "GC blocked because finding"
If a finding is found and the methodology can't be applied
cleanly (e.g., the audit becomes ambiguous), the GC child:
- Does NOT delete the memory file.
- Updates the MEMORY.md bullet to add `BLOCKED <date>: see
  <new-bead>`.
- Closes itself with reason `Blocked by audit finding; see
  <bead>`.

### What about "no findings AND nothing to GC"
Edge case: an entry in MEMORY.md was already manually edited
between session and this polish, no longer matches the format.
Child should:
- Detect: `grep -E '<topic-pattern>' MEMORY.md` returns nothing.
- Conclude: already GC'd.
- Close with reason `Already GC'd by external party; verified
  on <date>`.

### Logging
Each GC child run, regardless of outcome, emits:
```
{"phase":"gc","memory":"<topic>","outcome":"clean-deleted"|"finding-bead-filed"|"already-gone"|"blocked"}
```
